title: Pressed flower soy candle
subtitle: A clear-sided soy wax jar candle with dried botanicals pressed against the glass
excerpt: Dried and pressed flowers fixed to the inside of a glass jar before pouring create a botanical window effect visible through the wax. Soy wax at a soft container grade fills the jar and holds the botanicals in place. Scented with rose and geranium essential oil. The flowers must be fully dry before use.
sourceNotes: Pressed botanicals in candles: Bramble Berry candle techniques guide (2022). Soy container wax pour temperature and fragrance load: Candlescience technical reference (2022). Safety note on botanicals in candles: the botanicals are between the wax and the glass, not floating in the wax; this keeps them clear of the flame.

recipe.batchNotes: Scales easily to multiple jars. Press flowers onto the inside of each jar before the pour.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Flowers need to be fully dried and pressed before use. Use a flower press or a heavy book for 1 to 2 weeks before making the candle.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: The candle keeps several years. The pressed flower colours may fade slowly with exposure to light; store away from windows. The fragrance fades after around two years.

glossaryTerms.definition: The first time a candle is lit, burn it long enough for the wax pool to reach the full diameter of the jar. This sets the burn pattern for all subsequent uses.
The temperature at which wax is poured into the container. Soy container wax pours at 60 to 65 degrees C for the best adhesion to the glass.

content.text: A candle with pressed flowers or botanicals fixed between the wax and the glass gives a layered effect: the botanicals appear suspended in the wax when the candle is unlit, and the light from the flame makes them glow when lit. The key is fixing fully dried flowers against the inside of the jar before pouring. The botanicals are never embedded directly in the wax where they could catch the flame.
Ingredients

items.prepNote: soft container grade, e.g. 464 or equivalent
fully dried; suitable varieties include lavender, chamomile, cornflower, rose petals
ECO-4 or CD-12 for a 6 to 7 cm jar diameter

content.text: Method
Safety note

Melt wax over a water bath, not directly on a hob. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach. Do not embed botanicals loose in the wax body; only fix them against the inside wall of the glass, clear of the wax pool when the candle burns.
Prepare the jar and fix the botanicals

Warm the empty jar slightly with a hairdryer or in a low oven (50 degrees C for 5 minutes). Using a fine brush or toothpick, apply a small amount of melted wax to the inside of the glass where you want each flower to sit, then immediately press the dried flower flat against the glass and hold for 10 seconds until it sets. Work around the inside of the jar. The layer of wax poured afterwards holds the botanicals permanently in place.
Melt and cool the wax
Melt the soy wax over a water bath to 75 degrees C. Remove from heat and allow to cool to 60 to 65 degrees C. Stir in the rose geranium essential oil and mix for 2 minutes.
Set the wick and pour
Centre the pre-tabbed wick with a wick bar. Pour the wax gently at the
pour temperature
of 60 to 65 degrees C, pouring towards the centre to avoid disturbing the flowers pressed against the glass. Leave undisturbed to set at room temperature for at least 8 hours.
Second pour if needed
Soy wax sometimes develops a slight sink around the wick as it sets. If this happens, melt a small amount of wax, cool to 65 degrees C, and top up the centre only.
First burn
Trim the wick to 5 mm. On the first light, follow the
memory burn
rule and burn until the wax pool reaches the full jar diameter, around 2 to 3 hours for a 6 cm jar. Extinguishing the candle before the pool reaches the edge causes tunnelling on subsequent burns.
Storage and shelf life

Keep away from direct sunlight, which fades the flower colours. The candle keeps several years. Discard when the wax level is low enough that the jar itself begins to heat significantly during burning.
